verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is a soluble, secreted protein. It is intraacrosomal in sperm (not on the outer surface) and shows cytoplasmic localization in somatic testis cells. Although it has a specialized role in gamete fusion, it has no intrinsic membrane anchor (no TM, GPI, outer-leaflet lipidation, or stable TM-partner tethering) to maintain surface accessibility. No documented baseline or context-dependent outer-leaflet pool via its own mechanism. Ruled out contextual buckets: no cell-state-induction ecto-display (baseline intracellular/intraacrosomal, no stress/apoptosis/activation data); no tissue-restricted surface display (no anchor in testis, purely soluble); not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM cycling or dual-localization; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering.
